q.en: What gave Landon the idea to grow his own pumpkins?
q.zh: 是什么让兰登萌生了种南瓜的想法？

options.en: A newspaper ad reading "Get Your Seeds Now for Fall Harvest"
options.zh: 一则写着“现在就买种子，迎接秋季丰收”的报纸广告

explain.en: The book says a "bright newspaper ad caught his attention" with the words "Get Your Seeds Now for Fall Harvest."
explain.zh: 书中说一张“醒目的报纸广告吸引了他的注意”，上面写着“现在就买种子，迎接秋季丰收”。

q.en: By Halloween, how many bright orange pumpkins did Landon harvest?
q.zh: 到了万圣节，兰登收获了多少个鲜橙色的南瓜？

explain.en: The book says, "By Halloween, Landon had a dozen bright orange pumpkins."
explain.zh: 书中说：“到了万圣节，兰登有了一打鲜橙色的南瓜。”

q.en: Landon mixed compost into the soil to keep the plants healthy. The glossary defines "compost" as —
q.zh: 兰登把堆肥拌进土里让植物健康。词汇表把“compost（堆肥）”定义为——

options.en: A mixture of decaying organic material like vegetables and clippings
options.zh: 由蔬菜、剪枝等腐烂有机物混合而成的物质

explain.en: The glossary defines compost as "a mixture of decaying organic material, such as vegetables, plant clippings, or newspapers (p. 7)."
explain.zh: 词汇表把compost定义为“由蔬菜、植物剪枝或报纸等腐烂有机物混合而成的物质（第7页）”。

q.en: Landon's dad "tilled a small patch of ground in the garden." The glossary defines "tilled" as —
q.zh: 兰登的爸爸“tilled（翻耕）了花园里的一小块地”。词汇表把“tilled”定义为——

options.en: Broke and turned soil for planting
options.zh: 为种植而翻松、翻动土壤

explain.en: The glossary defines tilled as "broke and turned soil for planting (p. 7)."
explain.zh: 词汇表把tilled定义为“为种植而翻松、翻动土壤（第7页）”。

q.en: When a frost was forecast, how did Landon protect his tiny plants?
q.zh: 当预报有霜冻时，兰登如何保护他的小苗？

options.en: He cut the bottoms off plastic two-liter juice bottles and set them over the plants like greenhouses
options.zh: 他剪掉两升塑料果汁瓶的底部，罩在植物上像小温室

options.en: He covered them with a blanket

explain.en: The book says he "cut off the bottoms of the bottles... set one over each tiny plant like a miniature greenhouse."
explain.zh: 书中说他“剪掉瓶底……把瓶罩在每棵小苗上，像微型温室”。

q.en: After scooping out his pumpkins, what did Landon do with the seeds he found inside?
q.zh: 把南瓜挖空后，兰登如何处理在里面发现的种子？

options.en: He spread them on paper to dry and then roasted them as a snack
options.zh: 他把它们铺在纸上晾干，然后烤来当零食

options.en: He gave them to the dog

explain.en: The book says he "spread the seeds on paper to dry" and, following his dad's idea, decided "I can reuse them as a snack."
explain.zh: 书中说他“把种子铺在纸上晾干”，并听从爸爸的主意，决定“我可以把它们当零食再利用”。

q.en: Landon says, "Who would have thought I could use the three R's—reduce, reuse, and recycle—to grow pumpkins?" What is the author's purpose?
q.zh: 兰登说：“谁能想到我会用‘三个R’——减少、再利用、回收——来种南瓜呢？”作者的目的是什么？

options.en: To teach children about reducing, reusing, and recycling in a fun story
options.zh: 通过一个有趣的故事向孩子们传授减少、再利用和回收的理念

options.en: To explain how to cook soup

explain.en: The story weaves compost, reused bottles, and roasted seeds into the "three R's," showing the author's educational, pro-recycling purpose.
explain.zh: 故事把堆肥、再利用的瓶子和烤种子融入“三个R”，体现了作者寓教于乐、倡导回收的目的。

q.en: How did Landon share his dozen pumpkins?
q.zh: 兰登如何分配他的一打南瓜？

options.en: He sold six to neighbors, gave three to his brother Chad, and saved three
options.zh: 他卖了六个给邻居，给了弟弟查德三个，自己留了三个

explain.en: The book says, "He sold six to the neighbors, gave Chad three, and saved three for himself."
explain.zh: 书中说：“他卖了六个给邻居，给了查德三个，自己留了三个。”

q.en: Last Halloween Landon ruined his only carved pumpkin, but this year he grew many. What does comparing the two years show?
q.zh: 去年万圣节兰登弄坏了他唯一雕刻的南瓜，而今年他种了很多。把这两年作对比说明了什么？

options.en: Planning and hard work let him practice and succeed
options.zh: 计划和努力让他得以练习并取得成功

explain.en: Last year he "had no other pumpkin to carve"; this year he grew a dozen so "each pumpkin looked better than the one before."
explain.zh: 去年他“没有别的南瓜可刻”；今年他种了一打，于是“每个南瓜都比前一个刻得更好”。

q.en: On Halloween night, Landon was proud of six glowing jack-o'-lanterns on the porch. What is the author's final message?
q.zh: 万圣节之夜，兰登为门廊上六个发光的南瓜灯感到自豪。作者最后想传达什么？

options.en: Hard work and patience can lead to a proud, satisfying result
options.zh: 努力与耐心能带来令人自豪、满意的结果

explain.en: The book closes with Landon "proud of the six scary jack-o'-lanterns that glowed on the front porch," celebrating his effort.
explain.zh: 书的结尾是兰登“为门廊上六个发光的南瓜灯感到自豪”，赞美他的付出。

prompt.en: Landon used the three R's—reduce, reuse, and recycle—to grow his pumpkins. What is something you could reduce, reuse, or recycle, and how would you do it?
prompt.zh: 兰登用“三个R”——减少、再利用、回收——种出了南瓜。有什么东西你可以减少使用、再利用或回收？你会怎么做？

en: He put grass clippings, leaves, and food peels into a compost drum to make soil.
He reused cut-off plastic bottles as mini greenhouses for his plants.
He recycled the pumpkin seeds by drying and roasting them as a snack.
He made compost from the pumpkin insides for next year's garden.
zh: 他把草屑、落叶和食物皮放进堆肥桶制成土壤。
他把剪掉的塑料瓶再利用，当作植物的小温室。
他把南瓜籽晾干烤熟当零食，实现了回收。
他用南瓜内部废料做堆肥，留待明年花园使用。
